falsification_rule_text: Not supported if (a) synthetic-DiD effect on the primary outcome (parallel-market divergence or shortage incidence) is not positive and significant at 5% across episodes, OR (b) any single documented binding episode at policy-relevant scale (>=12 months duration, >= 1M population affected) produced measurable overall welfare improvement on the joint outcome vector (allocation efficiency + availability + quality + informal-market size) relative to its synthetic control.

variables_missing.name: price_ceiling_vs_parallel_market_ratio
variables_missing.source: constructed: official_price / parallel_market_price; coded per episode from academic sources

variables_missing.name: queue_time_or_shortage_incidence
variables_missing.source: constructed: episode-level documented waiting time / stockout frequency

variables_missing.name: quality_degradation_proxy
variables_missing.source: constructed: per-episode product-disappearance counts or consumer-survey quality decline

variables_missing.name: binding_price_ceiling_regime
variables_missing.source: constructed: binary indicator per country-year with episode start/end from policy chronologies

variables_missing.name: wartime_dummy
variables_missing.source: constructed: 1 if country-year is in an acknowledged war or immediate post-war demobilisation, else 0
